description: Wire the existing InputBar and ConversationView widgets into MarchApp and implement the AI conversation dispatch loop so users can type, submit, and receive streaming AI responses in the Textual TUI.

userStories.title: Wire real TUI widgets into MarchApp
userStories.description: As a user, I want the TUI input field to be interactive so that I can type messages and have the conversation view update.
userStories.notes: The real widgets (InputBar, ConversationView) already exist in tui_widgets/. This story is purely the wire-up: swap Static placeholders for the real ones and make the __init__ signatures forward **kwargs so id= can be passed.
implementationNotes: In src/mARCH/ui/tui_app.py compose(), replace Static('Conversation area…', id='conversation-area') with ConversationView(id='conversation-area') and replace Static('Input bar…', id='input-bar') with InputBar(id='input-bar')
Add on_mount(self) to MarchApp that calls self.query_one('#march-input', Input).focus() so the input is auto-focused when the app starts
Update src/mARCH/ui/tui_widgets/input_bar.py: change __init__(self) to __init__(self, **kwargs) and call super().__init__(**kwargs) — do not remove or rename any other attributes or methods
Update src/mARCH/ui/tui_widgets/conversation.py: same **kwargs forwarding change in __init__ — keep all existing methods (add_message, start_streaming, finish_streaming, _remove_placeholder) intact
Add imports to tui_app.py: from textual.widgets import Footer, Header, Input and from mARCH.ui.tui_widgets import ConversationView, InputBar — remove the Static import if it is no longer used
acceptanceCriteria: MarchApp.compose() yields a ConversationView instance (not a Static placeholder) with id='conversation-area'
MarchApp.compose() yields an InputBar instance (not a Static placeholder) with id='input-bar'
App mounts with the input field focused: query_one('#march-input') returns the focused widget
Existing test test_march_app_mounts_four_zones passes: #conversation-area and #input-bar selectors still resolve
InputBar.__init__ accepts **kwargs and forwards them to super().__init__(**kwargs)
ConversationView.__init__ accepts **kwargs and forwards them to super().__init__(**kwargs)
ruff check passes with no errors

userStories.title: AI conversation dispatch loop in MarchApp
userStories.description: As a user, I want to type a message, press Enter, and see my message displayed and then receive a streaming AI response in the TUI so I can have a real conversation with the agent.
userStories.notes: ConversationClient.stream_chat() is a sync generator — use @work(thread=True), not the default async worker. call_from_thread blocks the worker thread until the main-thread callback returns, which is intentional for getting start_streaming()'s MessageWidget return value. Do NOT import AppContext from cli.py — it would create a circular import.
implementationNotes: Add __init__(self, ai_client=None, agent=None, **kwargs) to MarchApp; store as self._ai_client and self._agent; call super().__init__(**kwargs); do NOT import AppContext from cli.py (circular import risk — cli.py already imports tui_app.py)

Add on_input_submitted(self, event: Input.Submitted) to MarchApp: strip text; return early if empty; call event.input.clear(); query ConversationView and call conv.add_message(MessageRole.USER, text); call self._agent.add_user_message(text) if self._agent is not None; if self._ai_client is not None start self._stream_ai_response(text), else call conv.add_message(MessageRole.ASSISTANT, 'AI client not configured — set ANTHROPIC_API_KEY')

Add @work(thread=True, exclusive=True) def _stream_ai_response(self, user_text: str): query ConversationView; call streaming_widget = self.call_from_thread(conv.start_streaming) to get the MessageWidget on the main thread; build messages from self._agent.get_conversation_context(include_system_prompt=True) if agent available, else use [{'role': 'user', 'content': user_text}]; iterate self._ai_client.stream_chat(messages) and for each chunk call self.call_from_thread(streaming_widget.append_chunk, chunk); call self.call_from_thread(conv.finish_streaming) on success; wrap body in try/except Exception as e and on error call self.call_from_thread(conv.add_message, MessageRole.ASSISTANT, f'Error: {e}') then self.call_from_thread(conv.finish_streaming)
Add imports to tui_app.py: from textual import work; from mARCH.ui.tui_widgets.message import MessageRole

Update src/mARCH/cli/cli.py: change MarchApp().run() to MarchApp(ai_client=ctx.ai_client, agent=ctx.agent).run() — ctx is already available from the get_app_context() call earlier in main(); only this one line changes in cli.py

Add unit tests in tests/test_tui_rewrite.py: (1) use async with MarchApp().run_test() pilot, simulate submitting text via pilot, assert a MessageWidget with MessageRole.USER appears in ConversationView and input is cleared; (2) assert that when ai_client=None an ASSISTANT message containing 'not configured' appears after submit
acceptanceCriteria: MarchApp.__init__ accepts optional ai_client and agent keyword arguments without breaking default no-arg construction
on_input_submitted handler exists on MarchApp and processes Input.Submitted events
Submitting non-empty text adds a USER MessageWidget to ConversationView and clears the input field
Submitting empty or whitespace-only text does NOT add a message to ConversationView
_stream_ai_response method is decorated with @work(thread=True) and accepts a str argument
When ai_client is None, submitting text adds an ASSISTANT message containing 'not configured' to ConversationView
src/mARCH/cli/cli.py::main passes ai_client and agent to MarchApp constructor
Unit test verifies: submitting text via pilot displays a USER MessageWidget in ConversationView
Unit test verifies: when ai_client=None, an ASSISTANT error message appears in ConversationView after submit
ruff check passes with no errors
